summary: Excellent soundboard capture with standout first‑set gems like The Loser, Bird Song and Music Never Stopped, plus a solid second set highlighted by Terrapin and Days Between.
review: The recording is consistently praised as crystal‑clear and “amazing,” earning five‑star marks for its sound quality. Musically the show shines in the first set – The Loser, Bird Song and Music Never Stopped receive glowing comments – while the second set delivers strong moments such as Terrapin, Days Between and a heartfelt Not Fade Away, despite a few weaker spots like Tom Thumb and Wave to the Wind.

ai_rating.rationale: Six detailed reviews overwhelmingly rate the sound as excellent (5‑star) and give the show mostly positive marks (average ~4‑star). Minor criticisms of a few songs keep the rating just below the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the audio “amazing,” “crisp,” and “excellent”; no complaints about drop‑outs or muddiness. The .sbd suffix confirms a soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is highlighted as the strongest portion of the concert, packed with high‑energy jams and vocal highlights. The second set maintains a solid groove, delivering memorable moments (Terrapin, Days Between) but lacks the peak intensity of the prior night’s marathon set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Raw, stripped‑down solo on The Loser; extended, creative playing on Bird Song and Music Never Stopped; chops noted as “up to speed” on Jack Straw; heartfelt vocal delivery on Days Between.
band_member_comments.Phil: Vocals described as a major highlight (The Loser, Wave to the Wind) and called "very welcome" and "incredible," though Wave to the Wind received mixed opinions about its strength.
band_member_comments.Bob: Leads into Saint and Terrapin with solid rhythm work; post‑drum “Need a Miracle” segment energized the crowd.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s synth tones were sometimes deemed too heavy, but his vocal contributions during choruses earned praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming characterized as psychedelic, "rainforest" space especially during Terrapin and the extended jams; post‑drum sections highlighted for crowd interaction.
